channel.title: Dan Patrick Show (Filtered)
channel.description: Daily DP Show episodes without C&R or Best Of clips.
channel.itunes:author: Dan Patrick

item.title: Hour 3 – Scott Fowler, Mel Kiper Jr.
item.description:
Charlotte Observer sportswriter Scott Fowler breaks down where things stand at UNC following the firing of Men’s Head Basketball Coach Hubert Davis, and previews where the direction of the head coaching search may go. NFL Draft analyst Mel Kiper Jr. sees this as a wide-open first round of the draft and confirms he has Fernando Mendoza as QB1 on his draft board. 

See omnystudio.com/listener for privacy information.
item.itunes:author: Dan Patrick

item.title: Hour 2 – ABS is Here, Darius Rucker
item.description:
Dan finds it hilarious that so many MLB players are losing inches in height after being officially measured prior to implementing the new Automated Ball/Strike system this season. And Grammy winning musician and friend of the program, Darius Rucker drops by to chop it up with DP.

See omnystudio.com/listener for privacy information.
item.itunes:author: Dan Patrick

item.title: Hour 1 – No Emmy for DP, Hubert Davis Fired, Nick Wright
item.description:
Dan and the Danettes lament being snubbed for the Sports Emmy Awards. They react to the firing of North Carolina Men’s Head Basketball Coach Hubert Davis. And Nick Wright stops by to rub their noses in his Sports Emmy nomination.

See omnystudio.com/listener for privacy information.
item.itunes:author: Dan Patrick

item.title: Hour 3 – Mac McClung, Dan Hurley
item.description:
All-time G-League leading scorer and three-time NBA dunk contest champion, Mac McClung joins the Dan Patrick Show and discusses what it’s like to take the scoring record, bouncing around on 10-day contracts with the NBA and fighting for roster spots, plus why other players didn’t want to go against him in the dunk contest. UConn Men’s Head Basketball Coach Dan Hurley sits down with Dan and explains how he’s never gotten a technical foul in an NCAA Tournament game despite his fiery reputation, and what Michigan State coach Tom Izzo told him when Hurley was considering the Lakers job.

See omnystudio.com/listener for privacy information.
item.itunes:author: Dan Patrick

item.title: Hour 2 – Unnecessary Dres Codes, Will Wade Back at LSU
item.description:
Dan and the Danettes spend the hour discussing a new dress code at a less than top tier steakhouse chain. Then it’s time to discuss airplane courtesy when it comes to dress, and callers weigh in on wearing sweatpants and taking off your shoes on the plane. LSU fires their basketball coach and brings back a familiar face in Will Wade.

See omnystudio.com/listener for privacy information.
item.itunes:author: Dan Patrick

item.title: Hour 1 – Too Much Netflix, Chase Daniel
item.description:
Dan wasn’t impressed by Netfilx’s handling of Major League Baseball’s Opening Night. Former NFL quarterback and current analyst Chase Daniel joins Dan to discuss comments from Dan Orlovsky about Alabama quarterback Ty Simpson, plus what Chase sees in Simpson and how he stacks up against Indiana QB Fernando Mendoza in the NFL Draft.

See omnystudio.com/listener for privacy information.
item.itunes:author: Dan Patrick

item.title: Hour 3 – Will the Real Iceman Please Stand Up, Robbie Hummel
item.description:
Former Purdue basketball star and current college hoops analyst Robbie Hummel joins Dan and talks about the moment when he realized Nebraska only had four men on the court against Iowa; plus stories about trying to guard Kevin Durant and playing basketball in Siberia. Bears quarterback Caleb Williams wants to be the Iceman, but George Gervin has something to say about that.

See omnystudio.com/listener for privacy information.
item.itunes:author: Dan Patrick

item.title: Hour 2 – Funeral Arrangements, Player vs. Coach Movement
item.description:
Dan and the Danettes slide off the rails a little and get into a discussion about being buried in your favorite team’s gear. And Dan thinks the NCAA needs to do a better job regulating the movement of coaches from school to school and making the rules more fair to the players.

See omnystudio.com/listener for privacy information.
item.itunes:author: Dan Patrick

item.title: Hour 1 – One Player Short, MLB Opening Day
item.description:
Dan and the Danettes recap last night’s Sweet 16 games and DP praises Nebraska HC Fred Hoiberg after he shoulders all the blame for only having four players on the court during a pivotal play in their loss to Iowa. And they recap Opening Day in Major League Baseball, including Netflix’s broadcast of Opening Night.

See omnystudio.com/listener for privacy information.
item.itunes:author: Dan Patrick

item.title: Hour 3 – Best and Worst of the Weekend, Women’s Tourney Tonight
item.description:
Dan and the Danettes take callers who share their best and worst of the weekend. Plus, DP weighs in on the Women’s Tourney as they play their last two Elite 8 games tonight.

See omnystudio.com/listener for privacy information.
item.itunes:author: Dan Patrick

item.title: Hour 2 – Tiger Woods Arrested, Seth Greenberg
item.description:
Dan has real concerns about Tiger Woods after his latest DUI arrest and worries that Woods might be a danger to himself or others if he’s allegedly having issues with substance abuse. And college hoops insider Seth Greenberg drops by to weigh in on UConn’s big win and the rest of the Final Four.

See omnystudio.com/listener for privacy information.
item.itunes:author: Dan Patrick

item.title: Hour 1 – UConn’s Comeback Win for the Ages
item.description:
There is really only one topic for Dan and the Danettes to focus on as the Duke Blue Devils blew a 19-point lead to the UConn Huskies and lost in dramatic fashion after appearing to have the game in hand.

See omnystudio.com/listener for privacy information.
item.itunes:author: Dan Patrick

item.title: Hour 3 – The DK Metcalf Rule, Reggie Miller
item.description:
Dan and the Danettes discuss the proposed rule changes in the NFL this season, including one being called the “DK Metcalf rule” about policing player vs. fan interaction during games. Plus, Reginald Aloysius Miller Jr. III drops by with an agenda.

See omnystudio.com/listener for privacy information.
item.itunes:author: Dan Patrick

item.title: Hour 2 – Deeper Dives, Jim “Bones” Mackay
item.description:
Dan and the Danettes dive deeper into the biggest stories of the day including Tiger Woods predicament, Dan Hurley’s headbutt, and Victor Wembanyama’s improving NBA MVP odds. Plus, longtime PGA caddy Jim “Bones” Mackay stops by to talk about what we can expect at Augusta next week and whether he thinks Tiger will attend and/or play The Masters.

See omnystudio.com/listener for privacy information.
item.itunes:author: Dan Patrick

item.title: Hour 1 – Tiger Tumult, Wemby Goes Off, Headbutt-Gate
item.description:
There are more questions than answers about Tiger Woods after he was arrested on suspicion of DUI and reports indicate he had opioids on him during the crash. Victor Wembanyama drops 41 points on the Bulls as his odds to win the NBA MVP Award just keep improving. And DP is perplexed by the video of UConn HC Dan Hurley seemingly headbutting a referee in the excitement following their game-winning shot.

See omnystudio.com/listener for privacy information.
item.itunes:author: Dan Patrick

item.title: Hour 3 – Grant Hill, Luka Dončić Scoring Machine
item.description:
Former Duke standout and current analyst Grant Hill drops by to share his personal tournament experiences and break down the Final Four matchups. And Dan is impressed by Luka Dončić scoring 600 points in the month of March to join an exclusive NBA club.

See omnystudio.com/listener for privacy information.
item.itunes:author: Dan Patrick

item.title: Hour 2 – Mike Florio, Tom Izzo
item.description:
NFL insider Mike Florio drops by as we are rapidly approaching the NFL Draft. And Michigan State Head Men’s Basketball Coach Tom Izzo joins the show and drops a bomb about some serious talks he had with the Phoenix Suns about their head coaching position last year.

See omnystudio.com/listener for privacy information.
item.itunes:author: Dan Patrick

item.title: Hour 1 – Final Four Preview, Tiger Steps Away
item.description:
Dan digs deep into the numbers and historical precedents surrounding the Final Four games scheduled for Saturday. Plus, he weighs in on Tiger Woods stepping away to treat his apparent addiction issues.

See omnystudio.com/listener for privacy information.
item.itunes:author: Dan Patrick

item.title: Hour 3 – Picture Day, More Details on Kirk Cousins
item.description:
Dan and the Danettes are gussied up for picture day in the man cave, and they recall some of their best and worst hair days. Plus, more details about Kirk Cousins heading to the Raiders and who is going to pay for it.

See omnystudio.com/listener for privacy information.
item.itunes:author: Dan Patrick

item.title: Hour 2 –Jalen Hurts Bashing, Jeremy Fowler
item.description:
Dan wonders about the timing of this negative story about Jalen Hurts considering it always seems to be something with the Philadelphia Eagles. NFL insider Jeremy Fowler joins Dan to weigh in on the Las Vegas Raiders’ acquisition of QB Kirk Cousins.

See omnystudio.com/listener for privacy information.
item.itunes:author: Dan Patrick

item.title: Hour 1 – Wemby Fatigue, Kirk Cousins in Vegas
item.description:
Dan scoffs at the idea that fans are getting tired of all the coverage of Victor Wembanyama and considers the chances this young San Antonio Spurs team might shock the world and win the NBA title. Plus, breaking news hits as Kirk Cousins heads to Las Vegas and changes a lot of speculation about the Raiders.

See omnystudio.com/listener for privacy information.
item.itunes:author: Dan Patrick

item.title: Hour 3 – Witnessing Sports History, Louis Riddick
item.description:
Dan waxes poetic about covering sports and how inspiring it is to be able to witness great moments. And Former NFL executive and current analyst Louis Riddick joins Dan and launches a passionate case for Fernando Mendoza as the top quarterback against recent criticism and talks about what he saw from him on Indiana’s pro day.

See omnystudio.com/listener for privacy information.
item.itunes:author: Dan Patrick

item.title: Hour 2 – Carter Jensen Takes a Nap, Chris Russo
item.description:
Dan chuckles after hearing the story of Royals C Carter Jensen oversleeping his alarm and missing the start of their game. Sports talk mogul Chris “Mad Dog” Russo joins Dan and reminisces about both of their careers and plans for a show together, plus he weighs in on the biggest chokes in sports history and the automated balls and strikes system in baseball.

See omnystudio.com/listener for privacy information.
item.itunes:author: Dan Patrick

item.title: Hour 1 – Final Four Preview, Fritzy Called Out
item.description:
Dan weighs in on the upcoming Final Four and the coaching changes we are likely to see after the tourney ends. Plus, Fritzy gets called short by the photographer yesterday and the Danettes determine if the photographer’s accusation is accurate.

See omnystudio.com/listener for privacy information.
item.itunes:author: Dan Patrick

item.title: Hour 3 – North Carolina Left at the Altar, Guy Fieri
item.description:
North Carolina’s not the job it used to be, as coaches seem more interested in using the school as a threat to get bigger deals from their existing schools rather than treating the Tarheels like a blue blood job. Dan gets name-dropped in a song. “Diners, Drive-ins and Dives” host Guy Fieri joins the show to brag about his position in the DP Celebrity Bracket Challenge.

See omnystudio.com/listener for privacy information.
item.itunes:author: Dan Patrick

item.title: Hour 2 – Geno’s Apology Rings Hollow, Ray Allen
item.description:
UConn Women’s Head Basketball Coach Geno Auriemma’s apology rings hollow to Dan because South Carolina’s coach Dawn Staley isn’t mentioned at all. Former NBA and UConn basketball star Ray Allen joins Dan and discusses how the Huskies can attempt to bring down Michigan in the title games, his own memories playing at UConn and how basketball mentality has changed – also don’t make fun of his t-shirt.

See omnystudio.com/listener for privacy information.
item.itunes:author: Dan Patrick

item.title: Hour 1 – Geno Auriemma Lost His Cool, Ian Eagle
item.description:
The greatest coach in women’s basketball history lost his composure and acted in an embarrassing manner against South Carolina’s Dawn Staley during the Women’s Final Four. CBS Sports broadcaster Ian Eagle joins Dan to talk about Monday night’s matchup between Michigan and UConn for the men’s title, encountering a robot with his food, and Maury Povich.

See omnystudio.com/listener for privacy information.
item.itunes:author: Dan Patrick

item.title: Hour 3 – Jalen Rose, Reggie Miller
item.description:
Michigan legend and member of the fabled “Fab Five,” Jalen Rose stops by to bask in the glow of the Wolverines National Championship. And Hall of Famer Reggie Miller swings by to give his 2 cents on last night’s game and talk a little NBA as well.

See omnystudio.com/listener for privacy information.
item.itunes:author: Dan Patrick

item.title: Hour 2 – Dusty May, Seton Evolves
item.description:
Michigan Wolverines Head Men’s Basketball Coach Dusty May joins the show after winning the National Championship last night. And longtime Danette Seton O’Connor makes an announcement about his future with the radio program.

See omnystudio.com/listener for privacy information.
item.itunes:author: Dan Patrick

item.title: Hour 1 – Michigan Crowned, Jay Bilas
item.description:
Dan reacts to the Michigan Wolverines winning the NCAA Men’s Basketball Tournament and capturing the National Championship over the UConn Huskies. And college basketball insider Jay Bilas drops by to give his thoughts on last night’s game and the future of college basketball.

See omnystudio.com/listener for privacy information.
item.itunes:author: Dan Patrick
